competencies.competency_text: Connects changes in the environment and the lives of their family and community, as communicated by elders and through local stories (changes in occupation, food habits, resources, celebrations, communication)
competencies.justification: 'How do we Brush our Teeth?' requires students to interview grandparents or elderly neighbours about what they used to wash hair, body and clothes and clean their teeth when young, and whether these things can still be found — directly connecting past resource-use (datun, reetha, shikakai) to present practices through elder knowledge.
sections: How do we Brush our Teeth?

competencies.competency_text: Describes the basic safety needs and protection (health and hygiene, food, water, shelter, precautions, awareness of emergency situations, abuse, and unsafe situations) of humans, birds, and animals
competencies.justification: 'Clean and Bright' is architecturally built around health and hygiene as a basic safety need — brushing teeth twice, washing hands after toilet use, showering, trimming nails. 'Playing Safely' extends this to awareness of unsafe situations (busy road, roof without railings, construction site, stranger interactions).

competencies.competency_text: Develops simple labels and slogans, and participates in roleplay on safety and protection in the local environment to be displayed/done in school and locality
competencies.justification: 'Playing Safely' presents three comic-dialogue scenarios where children roleplay refusing sweets from a stranger, declining to be picked up by an unknown person, and refusing a lift in a car — direct roleplay on personal safety and protection in the local environment.

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes cultural diversity in their immediate environment (food, clothing, games, different seasons, festivals related to harvest and sowing)
competencies.justification: 'Playing Outdoors and Indoors too' — Activity 2 and the 'Name the Game!' Find out task present traditional Indian games (spinning top, ludo, snakes-and-ladders, carrom) and a word-search containing kabaddi, kho-kho, cricket, football, badminton, hockey, etc. — requiring students to identify cultural diversity in games in their immediate environment.

competencies.competency_text: Presents observations and findings through different creative modes (drawing, diagram, poem, play, skit, oral and written expression)
competencies.justification: 'Let us reflect' — A.Write requires students to fill a structured weekly health tracking table (written data recording); B.Draw requires students to draw a 24-slice circle diagram representing a full day's activities — presenting self-observations through tabular and diagrammatic creative modes.
